name: I fell off a ladder at work yesterday and my back hurts bad, what do I do right now?

mainEntity.name: I fell off a ladder at work yesterday and my back hurts bad, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Go to the doctor immediately, report to your boss in writing, take photos, and don't talk to insurance without a lawyer.[3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Seek immediate medical attention** to document your injuries and begin treatment, then report the incident to your supervisor in writing.[3]

Workers' compensation typically covers medical bills and lost wages for on-the-job ladder falls on a no-fault basis.[1][2] Delaying care can weaken your claim.

- Get checked by a doctor even if pain seems minor.
- Take photos of the ladder, site, and your injuries.
- Note witnesses and get their contact info.

File a workers' comp claim promptly through your employer; consult a lawyer before speaking to insurance adjusters.[3] A specialist can identify if third-party claims apply for fuller compensation.

description: Go to the doctor immediately, report to your boss in writing, take photos, and don't talk to insurance without a lawyer.[3]
